summary:
The Turku sugar study primarily investigated the relationship between sugar intake and dental caries development. Frequent sugary snacks were found to significantly increase the risk of cavities more so than occasional sweets consumed at mealtimes. To prevent these issues, researchers aimed to promote better diet-related practices among participants. Agent
The Turku sugar study concentrated on understanding how frequently eating sugar affects dental caries; findings showed higher risks for cavities with multiple small sugar exposures daily versus fewer larger intakes at meals. This led to recommendations promoting healthier diets to reduce cavity formation.
